behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide." 
Slot B: Boundary is a pure key-derivation algorithm on input parameters, with no required module/I-O boundary. 
Slot C: Lower than Unit is impossible; only a direct function/object test can prove determinism and collision resistance cases here. 
Rejected higher level: no behavior text requires DB, filesystem, network, subprocess, or AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the key builder with canonicalized parameter permutations: assert equal params -> equal key, and a large adversarial set of distinct params -> distinct keys.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "TTL ... computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule." 
Slot B: Failure boundary is local mapping/lookup logic from category -> TTL value. 
Slot C: Lower than Unit cannot exist; this is direct computation validation, not cross-module state handoff. 
Rejected Integration/System: text does not require real config service, DB, OS timer behavior, or external I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test TTL computation with an in-memory freshness-rule map; verify exact durations per category and default/fallback behavior.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches ... as specified in the configuration." 
Slot B: Failure appears at module handoff: memory-usage source + config reader + strategy selector must agree on threshold interpretation. 
Slot C: Unit of selector alone can miss wiring bugs (wrong config key/units, stale metric propagation) that only appear when modules are connected. 
Integration-brake check: no required real OS/network/subprocess, no AI output, no multi-agent chain. Rejected System by text scope (local threshold/config switching logic).
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test wired cache manager with real in-process modules (metric provider, config loader, selector): drive usage below/above threshold and assert active policy changes LRU -> LFU exactly at configured boundary.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries ..." 
Slot B: Boundary includes real DB write/commit semantics and invalidation propagation from PostgreSQL into cache state. 
Slot C: Integration with mocked DB can hide real failures (trigger/listener timing, transaction visibility, SQL-level row matching) that only surface with real Postgres behavior. 
Rejected lower level: behavior explicitly anchors invalidation to underlying PostgreSQL writes, so in-process fakes may miss true failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: System-test against a real PostgreSQL instance: prefill cache for target rows, execute real INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on table, then verify all and only matching cache keys are invalidated after commit.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "eviction policy removes the correct entries ... under LRU ... under LFU ... when the cache is full." 
Slot B: Core failure boundary is eviction data-structure/algorithm correctness under controlled access patterns. 
Slot C: Unit is the floor; testing only broader wiring can miss algorithmic ordering/count bugs and ties behavior. 
Rejected Integration/System: no explicit requirement for external I/O; policy correctness can be falsified entirely in-memory.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test cache policy objects with deterministic operation traces: for LRU verify recency order eviction; for LFU verify frequency-based eviction (including tie-break rules) at capacity.
